channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: DOGE structure, authority emerge as biggest DC mystery under Trump
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) structure and authority has become a central mystery in the barrage of lawsuits seeking to stymie its operations.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Samsung Galaxy tri-fold – 5 key rumored features, from its screen size to its hinge
item.description: Samsung has only hinted at the existence of a tri-fold Galaxy phone, but that hasn’t stopped tipsters and leakers from providing us with a wealth of rumors about the supposed new addition to the Samsung lineup.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Netflix has 8 new movies and shows with 100% on Rotten Tomatoes so far in 2025 – here they are
item.description: Stream the best new movies and shows on Netflix in 2025 according to Rotten Tomatoes with animation, drama, true crime and more.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Two AI developer strategies: Hire engineers or let AI do the work
item.description: The stark difference in the way tech giants in China and the US are approaching AI for internal operations was illustrated late this week by separate announcements from Salesforce and Alibaba.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Fryer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thousands of misconfigured building access systems have been leaked online
item.description: Crooks could access buildings that should be off limits, or even exploit them to steal sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: MasOrange acelera su transformación digital avivada por la IA mediante una alianza con Google Cloud
item.description: A las puertas de la celebración de una nueva edición del Mobile World Congress (MWC), MasOrange anuncia una nueva alianza estratégica.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Stripe hits $91.5 billion valuation in latest tender offer
item.description: Stripe on Thursday announced a tender offer for employees and shareholders that valued the company at $91.5 billion, nearly 41% higher than its valuation a year ago, potentially delaying the fintech firm’s ambitions of going public.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: StarlingX 10.0 Released: A Leap Forward for Distributed Clouds and Edge Computing
item.description: The Open Infrastructure Foundation has released StarlingX 10.0, a significant update to the open-source distributed cloud platform designed for IoT, 5G, O-RAN, and edge computing applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Vietnam plans energy shift toward building more solar, less reliance on gas and coal
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam is revising its energy plans to focus more on large solar farms and less on reliance on coal and natural gas. The fast-growing economy now aims to get 16% of its energy from the sun — more than triple its earlier target of just 5%.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What’s next for Microsoft’s Semantic Kernel
item.description: At the heart of Microsoft’s AI application development strategy is Semantic Kernel, an open source set of tools for managing and orchestrating AI prompts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Apple’s Vision Pro enters Industry 4.0 with Dassault Systèmes
item.description: Are you ready for Vision Pro week? Well, you should be as hot on the heels of some highly significant enterprise-focused visionOS improvements, Apple has entered a new initiative that puts its device at the heart of future manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: As Microsoft innovates, Apple stagnates
item.description: Microsoft and Apple were once the yin and yang of the tech world. Microsoft was dull and plodding, milking buggy, barely-good-enough-for-prime-time Windows for tremendous profits, thanks to a worldwide operating system monopoly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 11 rules for writing better code
item.description: You can’t develop software and manage software projects for 30 years and not learn a thing or two or twelve about writing good code. Here are a near-dozen nuggets of coding wisdom I picked up along the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI, automation spur efforts to upskill network pros
item.description: Networking skills are evolving to keep pace with broader technology trends, and network pros need to proactively develop new competencies in areas such as AI and automation to stay relevant.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Compute Exchange aims to disrupt AI compute access with auction-based platform
item.description: Companies looking for additional compute capacity for their AI initiatives at a price they can afford now have another option. Compute Exchange offers what it calls the world’s first auction-based exchange for AI compute resources.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New European AI weather model to improve forecasts
item.description: The European Center for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) has made its new AI model, the Artificial Intelligence Forecasting System (AIFS), available to everyone free of charge. Medium-range weather forecasts are those that predict weather conditions for three to 15 days into the future.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: I’m a tech expert. These are the 5 time-saving tech tips I tell everyone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson presents five secret shortcuts: Adjust sound/vibration, share YouTube moments, fix tabs and tweak brightness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: This is what they never told you about online privacy
item.description: Understanding the language of the internet can help you protect your personal information from bad actors. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
